papers.title: OpenKedge: Governing Agentic Mutation with Execution-Bound Safety and Evidence Chains
papers.innovation: Introduces OpenKedge, a protocol that redefines state mutation by autonomous AI agents as a governed process, rather than direct execution. It emphasizes execution-bound safety and evidence chains to provide sufficient context, coordination, and safety guarantees, addressing fundamental flaws in API-centric architectures for agent deployment.
papers.benchmarks: Not specified in abstract; describes a conceptual protocol and framework for agent governance.
papers.use_case: Developing and deploying autonomous AI agents in critical environments where safety, auditability, and controlled execution of actions (state mutations) are paramount. Essential for ensuring agent reliability and preventing unintended side effects.

papers.title: From Business Events to Auditable Decisions: Ontology-Governed Graph Simulation for Enterprise AI
papers.innovation: Proposes an ontology-governed graph simulation approach to ground LLM-based agent decisions within active business scenarios. This method aims to produce auditable and contextually relevant decisions, addressing the common problem of LLM agents generating fluent but ungrounded responses lacking an audit trail in enterprise settings.
papers.benchmarks: Not specified in abstract; describes an architectural approach for enterprise AI systems.
papers.use_case: Building robust, explainable, and auditable AI agent systems for enterprise applications. Crucial for industries requiring strict compliance, transparency, and logical grounding of AI-driven decisions within complex business processes.

papers.title: SPPO: Sequence-Level PPO for Long-Horizon Reasoning Tasks
papers.innovation: Introduces Sequence-Level PPO (SPPO), an optimization framework designed to overcome the instability of temporal credit assignment and the prohibitively high computational cost of standard token-level PPO when aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) for long-horizon Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning tasks.
papers.benchmarks: Implicitly, improvements in stability and efficiency on long-horizon reasoning tasks and Chain-of-Thought (CoT) tasks, compared to token-level PPO's performance in these settings.
papers.use_case: Training and fine-tuning LLMs for complex, multi-step reasoning problems. AI engineers can leverage SPPO to achieve more stable and computationally efficient alignment of LLMs for advanced agentic behaviors and problem-solving, especially when dealing with long context windows and detailed reasoning paths.
